branch.name: fix/infrastructure-hardening
branch.priority: P0 — do first (quick wins, unblocks confidence)
branch.summary: Complete Docker enforcement plan (#424) — 95% already done. Wire pre-push git hook (#469) — script exists, needs hook. Add Prefect decoupling quadruple-check verification commit.

tasks.task: T-01.1: Close enforcement gaps (T-08 smoke test annotation, T-13 admin)
T-01.2: Wire git pre-push hook calling pr_readiness_check.sh
T-01.3: Add Prefect decoupling verification test (quadruple-check)
T-01.4: Push, PR, close #424 and #469

branch.risk: LOW — mostly verification and wiring of existing code

branch.name: feat/monai-ecosystem-audit
branch.summary: Audit ModelAdapter ABC for MONAI-native compatibility. Re-add 4 MONAI model thin wrappers (SegResNet, SwinUNETR, UNETR, AttentionUnet). Refactor model factory from if/elif dispatch to registry dict. Create model profile configs. Document template adapter pattern.

tasks.task: T-02.1: Audit and slim ModelAdapter ABC if needed
T-02.2: Refactor model_builder.py to registry dict pattern
T-02.3: Add SegResNet thin wrapper + tests + profile
T-02.4: Add SwinUNETR thin wrapper + tests + profile
T-02.5: Add UNETR thin wrapper + tests + profile
T-02.6: Add AttentionUnet thin wrapper + tests + profile
T-02.7: Verify MONAI losses/inferers work with all adapters
T-02.8: Template adapter documentation
T-02.9: Push, PR, close #474 and #343

branch.risk: MEDIUM — new code, but following well-established DynUNet pattern
branch.guardrails: Each adapter MUST be under 150 lines. If it exceeds that, the ABC needs fixing. Use MONAI native constructors — zero custom architecture code. Every adapter needs: instantiation test, forward pass test, config test.

branch.name: feat/test-tiers
branch.summary: Split 3800+ tests into staging (fast, PR default) vs prod (full, nightly). Apply pytest markers consistently. Update pytest config. Create scripts/run_staging_tests.sh and scripts/run_prod_tests.sh.

tasks.task: T-03.1: Audit test timing — identify slow tests (>5s each)
T-03.2: Apply @pytest.mark.slow to integration/slow tests
T-03.3: Create staging vs prod pytest configs
T-03.4: Update pr_readiness_check.sh to use staging tier
T-03.5: Push, PR, close #304

branch.risk: LOW — no code changes, only test configuration

branch.name: feat/training-resume-hpo
branch.summary: Fix auto-resume bug (fold_count vs n_folds). Add integration test. Implement train_all_hyperparam_combos.sh via Docker + hpo_flow. Create experiment YAML configs for grid sweeps.

tasks.task: T-04.1: Fix run_training_flow.py bug (fold_count → n_folds)
T-04.2: Add auto-resume integration test with mock MLflow
T-04.3: Create experiment configs (configs/experiments/*.yaml)
T-04.4: Implement train_all_hyperparam_combos.sh via Docker
T-04.5: Connect scripts/run_hpo.py to hpo_flow deployment
T-04.6: Push, PR, close #365 and #401

branch.risk: MEDIUM — training integration requires careful Docker volume testing
branch.guardrails: Scripts MUST use docker compose run, NEVER bare uv run python. Auto-resume must preserve MLflow fingerprint stability. HPO grid reads from YAML — no hardcoded combinations in bash.

verification.name: prefect-decoupling-quadruple-check
verification.description: Woven into Branch 1. Verified by 4 subagents on 2026-03-08. Result: ZERO violations across all 12 flows. Scorecard: all flows have docker gates, no cross-imports, MLflow-only communication, explicit volume mounts.
verification.status: VERIFIED — see Agent analysis in session transcript

execution-instructions.prerequisite: 1. Ensure main branch is up to date: git checkout main and git pull 2. Ensure uv sync has been run recently 3. Ensure no uncommitted changes on main
execution-instructions.run-command:
claude -p "Read /home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/minivess-mlops/docs/planning/overnight-child-01.xml and execute it autonomously. Follow ALL instructions in the file. Commit after each phase. When complete, push and create the PR, then stop."
for i in 01 02 03 04; do claude -p "Read and execute /home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/minivess-mlops/docs/planning/overnight-child-$i.xml autonomously. Follow ALL instructions. Commit after each phase. Push, create PR, close issues when done. Then stop." done
execution-instructions.failure-handling: If a child plan fails mid-execution: 1. The branch will have partial commits (safe — no force push) 2. The next child plan starts fresh from main (no dependency) 3. Review the failed branch manually in the morning 4. Each child plan is independent — later plans still work
execution-instructions.context-management: Each child plan is designed for a FRESH context window: - No assumption of prior session state - Full file paths provided (no "the file we edited earlier") - Acceptance criteria are self-verifiable (pytest commands included) - Each plan starts with "git checkout main and git pull"
